conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to ask:

Are you the person responsible for Alex? This means you're the main person who looks after Alex and pays for things like food, clothes, and other expenses.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't actually live with me at the moment. They live elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Can you tell me more about Alex's living situation? Specifically:

1. Who does Alex live with currently?
2. Do you still contribute financially to Alex's upbringing (such as paying for food, clothes, or other expenses)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent. And no, I'm not currently contributing financially to their upbringing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I need to understand a bit more about your situation.

Does Alex ever live with you for periods of time, or do they live with their other parent full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They live with their other parent full-time. Alex doesn't live with me at all.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you must be "responsible for the child." According to UK government rules, this means you need to either:
- Live with the child, OR
- Contribute to the cost of supporting them (at least the equivalent amount of what Child Benefit would pay)

In your situation:
- Alex lives with their other parent full-time and doesn't live with you
- You don't contribute financially to Alex's upbringing

Since you neither live with Alex nor provide financial support, you don't meet the requirement of being "responsible for the child," and therefore you're not eligible to claim Child Benefit.

The other parent who Alex lives with would likely be the eligible person to claim Child Benefit, as they are the one responsible for Alex's care and expenses.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: To be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, you must be responsible for the child. This means you need to live with the child OR contribute to the cost of supporting them (at least the same as Child Benefit would pay for that child). In this case: (1) Alex lives with their other parent full-time and does not live with you at all, (2) You do not contribute financially to Alex's upbringing. Since you neither live with Alex nor contribute financially to their support, you do not meet the 'responsible for the child' requirement and therefore are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has an 8-year-old child named Alex who lives full-time with their other parent. The claimant does not live with Alex and does not contribute financially to Alex's upbringing. Under UK Child Benefit rules, to be eligible, a person must be responsible for the child, which requires either living with the child or contributing at least the equivalent of what Child Benefit would pay towards the child's costs. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
